CHANGELOG.html.title: Changelog | DocTools
CHANGELOG.html.summary: Changelog [0.2.0] — 2026-03-18 Added package.json — formalizes DocTools as a versioned package (com.wayngames.doctools) with display name, description, and author metadata. Changed Flattened Documentation/ layout — moved all pages out of Documentation/Manual/ into Documentation/ directly; updated toc.md and index.md links accordingly. Removed Documentation/doctools.config (sample config that lived inside the docs folder). Fixed Version dropdown now shows the human-readable display name (e.g. "DocTools 0.2.0") instead of the technical package name. [0.1.0] — 2025-03-18 Initial release of DocTools. Features init command — Scaffold a Documentation/ directory with doctools.config, root toc.md, index.md, and a Manual/ folder with starter pages. build command — Build documentation for a single version or all versions at once. Runs docfx metadata (for the current version) then docfx build with the custom template. version command — Manage documentation versions: list, add, remove, set-current. Adding a version snapshots the current TOC and index. snapshot command — Copy the current root toc.md and index.md into the active version directory. doctools.config — Single-file configuration covering package metadata, version lists, per-version content overrides (VERSION_CONTENT), extra content files, and resource patterns. Multi-version output — Each version builds into its own v{version}/ subdirectory with a generated versions.js and root redirect. WaynGames.StaticToc.Extension template — Custom DocFX template extending statictoc with: Version dropdown in the breadcrumb bar Dark / light theme toggle with localStorage persistence and system-preference detection Mermaid diagram rendering Syntax highlighting via Highlight.js WCAG AA–compliant color palette for both themes Responsive layout with print stylesheet Reduced-motion and forced-colors (Windows High Contrast) support

index.html.title: DocTools | DocTools
index.html.summary: DocTools DocTools is a Bash CLI for building multi-version DocFX documentation sites. It handles scaffolding, version management, and builds — producing a static site with a version switcher, dark/light theme toggle, and Mermaid diagram support, all powered by the WaynGames.StaticToc.Extension template. Key Features One-command setup — doctools.sh init scaffolds a complete Documentation/ directory with config, TOC, and starter pages. Multi-version builds — Each version gets its own output folder with a global version dropdown. Per-version content overrides — The VERSION_CONTENT config lets each version pull from different source directories or exclude specific files. Custom template — Dark/light theme, Mermaid diagrams, syntax highlighting, responsive layout, and WCAG AA accessibility. Minimal configuration — A single doctools.config file with sensible defaults. Manual Page Description Getting Started Prerequisites, installation, first build Configuration Full doctools.config reference Commands All CLI commands and flags Versioning Multi-version workflow and snapshots Template WaynGames.StaticToc.Extension overview Prerequisites Requirement Notes Bash (or Git Bash on Windows) DocTools scripts are pure Bash DocFX 2.x Must be on PATH — run docfx --version to verify .NET 9+ Required by DocFX for metadata extraction

manual/commands.html.title: Commands | DocTools
manual/commands.html.summary: Commands DocTools provides four commands, all invoked through doctools.sh. doctools.sh <command> <package-dir> [arguments] init Scaffold a Documentation/ directory for a package. doctools.sh init <package-dir> [options] Options Flag Description Default --name NAME Package name Directory basename --title TITLE Documentation title Same as --name --version VER Initial version 0.1.0 --footer FOOTER Footer HTML Empty Example doctools.sh init ./MyPackage --name "My Package" --title "My Package Docs" --version "1.0.0" --footer "© 2025" Creates: MyPackage/Documentation/ ├── doctools.config ├── toc.md ├── index.md └── Manual/ ├── toc.md ├── getting-started.md └── Images/ build Build documentation for one or all versions. doctools.sh build <package-dir> [version|all] all (default) — Builds every version in ALL_VERSIONS. The current version runs docfx metadata then docfx build; historical versions run docfx build only. Also generates versions.js and a root index.html redirect. <version> — Builds a single version. If it matches CURRENT_VERSION, metadata extraction runs first. Example # Build all versions doctools.sh build ./MyPackage all # Build only version 0.2.0 doctools.sh build ./MyPackage 0.2.0 Build Pipeline Load config — Sources Documentation/doctools.config, validates required fields, sets defaults. Metadata (current version only) — Runs docfx metadata to extract API docs from C# source into .tmp/api/. Generate docfx.json — Creates a temporary docfx.json with content entries, resource patterns, template paths, and global metadata. Build — Runs docfx build, outputting to {OUTPUT_DIR}/v{version}/. Output generation — Writes versions.js and (when building all) a root redirect index.html. Global Metadata Every page receives these metadata values: Key Value _appTitle PACKAGE_TITLE _appFooter PACKAGE_FOOTER _appName PACKAGE_NAME _appLogoPath LOGO_PATH _appFaviconPath FAVICON_PATH _disableContribution true _enableSearch true _disableNextArticle true _lang "en" _packageVersion Current build version version Manage documentation versions. doctools.sh version <package-dir> <action> [version] Actions list List all versions. The current version is marked with *. doctools.sh version ./MyPackage list 0.2.0 * 0.1.0 add Add a new version. It becomes the current version and is prepended to ALL_VERSIONS. The current toc.md and index.md are copied into a v{version}/ directory. doctools.sh version ./MyPackage add 0.3.0 remove Remove a version from the config. You cannot remove the current version — use set-current first. doctools.sh version ./MyPackage remove 0.1.0 set-current Set which version is treated as current (receives metadata extraction on next build). doctools.sh version ./MyPackage set-current 0.2.0 snapshot Copy the root toc.md and index.md into the current version's directory (v{CURRENT_VERSION}/). Use this to archive the table of contents before making changes for a new version. doctools.sh snapshot <package-dir> Example doctools.sh snapshot ./MyPackage Copies Documentation/toc.md and Documentation/index.md into Documentation/v{CURRENT_VERSION}/.

manual/configuration.html.title: Configuration | DocTools
manual/configuration.html.summary: Configuration All DocTools settings for a package live in a single file: Documentation/doctools.config. This file is a Bash script that is sourced during the build, so standard Bash syntax applies. Field Reference Required Fields Field Type Description PACKAGE_NAME string Package identifier (used internally and in metadata) PACKAGE_TITLE string Human-readable title shown in the navbar and page titles CURRENT_VERSION string The version that receives C# metadata extraction during builds ALL_VERSIONS array All versions to build, newest first. Example: ("0.3.0" "0.2.0" "0.1.0") Optional Fields Field Type Default Description PACKAGE_FOOTER string "" Footer HTML rendered at the bottom of every page METADATA_SRC string ".." Path (relative to Documentation/) to the directory containing C# source files LOGO_PATH string "" Path to logo image displayed in the navbar FAVICON_PATH string "" Path to the site favicon OUTPUT_DIR string "../Documentation~" Build output directory (relative to Documentation/) METADATA_ALLOW_COMPILATION_ERRORS string "true" Allow docfx metadata to succeed even when C# has compilation errors METADATA_NAMESPACE_LAYOUT string "nested" Namespace layout in generated API docs: "nested" or "flat" EXTRA_CONTENT array () Additional files to include in every version build (paths relative to Documentation/) RESOURCE_PATTERNS array () Glob patterns for non-markdown resources (images, etc.). Defaults to Manual/**/*.png if empty VERSION_CONTENT VERSION_CONTENT is a Bash associative array that lets you override content sources on a per-version basis. Declare it as: declare -A VERSION_CONTENT VERSION_CONTENT["0.1.0"]="manual_src|manual_exclude|toc_src" Each value is a pipe-delimited string with three components: Component Default Description manual_src Manual Source directory for manual pages (relative to Documentation/) manual_exclude Draft/**,Draft V2/** Comma-separated glob patterns to exclude from the manual toc_src . Directory containing toc.md and index.md for this version Leave a component empty to keep the default. For example, to only override the exclude patterns: VERSION_CONTENT["0.2.0"]="|Internal/**|" Example A complete doctools.config for a Unity package with two versions: PACKAGE_NAME="com.wayngames.mypkg" PACKAGE_TITLE="My Package" PACKAGE_FOOTER="© 2025 WAYN Games" METADATA_SRC=".." LOGO_PATH="" FAVICON_PATH="" OUTPUT_DIR="../Documentation~" CURRENT_VERSION="0.2.0" ALL_VERSIONS=("0.2.0" "0.1.0") declare -A VERSION_CONTENT VERSION_CONTENT["0.1.0"]="Manual||v0.1.0" EXTRA_CONTENT=("../LICENSE.md" "../CHANGELOG.md") RESOURCE_PATTERNS=("Manual/**/*.png" "Manual/**/*.gif") METADATA_ALLOW_COMPILATION_ERRORS=true METADATA_NAMESPACE_LAYOUT="nested"

manual/getting-started.html.title: Getting Started | DocTools
manual/getting-started.html.summary: Getting Started This guide walks you through installing DocTools, initializing documentation for a package, and running your first build. Prerequisites Requirement Notes Bash (or Git Bash on Windows) All DocTools scripts are pure Bash DocFX 2.x Must be on PATH. Verify with docfx --version .NET 9+ Required by DocFX for C# metadata extraction Installation Clone the DocTools repository: git clone <your-doctools-repo-url> /path/to/DocTools No additional installation is needed. Run commands directly via doctools.sh. Tip Add the DocTools directory to your PATH so you can call doctools.sh from anywhere. Initialize a Package Run init to scaffold a Documentation/ directory inside your package: doctools.sh init ./MyPackage --name "My Package" --title "My Package Docs" This creates: MyPackage/ └── Documentation/ ├── doctools.config ├── toc.md ├── index.md └── Manual/ ├── toc.md ├── getting-started.md └── Images/ See Commands — init for all available flags. Build the Documentation Build all versions (in this case just the initial one): doctools.sh build ./MyPackage all Output goes to MyPackage/Documentation~/ by default: MyPackage/ └── Documentation~/ ├── index.html # Redirect to current version ├── versions.js # Version list for the dropdown └── v0.1.0/ ├── index.html ├── manual/ └── styles/ View the Output Open the generated site in your browser: start MyPackage/Documentation~/v0.1.0/index.html # Windows open MyPackage/Documentation~/v0.1.0/index.html # macOS xdg-open MyPackage/Documentation~/v0.1.0/index.html # Linux Next Steps Configuration — Customize doctools.config for your package. Versioning — Add versions and manage snapshots. Commands — Full command reference.

manual/template.html.title: Template | DocTools
manual/template.html.summary: Template DocTools ships with the WaynGames.StaticToc.Extension template, which extends the built-in DocFX statictoc template with custom styling, a version switcher, theme toggling, and diagram support. Features Feature Description Version dropdown A <select> in the breadcrumb bar, populated from versions.js, that navigates between version builds Dark / light theme toggle Button in the navbar. Persists the user's choice in localStorage (key: doctools-theme) and respects the OS prefers-color-scheme preference on first visit Mermaid diagrams Mermaid v11.11.0 is loaded automatically. Use fenced code blocks with the mermaid language tag Syntax highlighting Highlight.js with DotnetConfig language support WCAG AA accessibility All color combinations meet 4.5:1 contrast for text and 3:1 for UI elements in both themes Responsive layout Sidebar collapses on narrow viewports; print stylesheet hides navigation Reduced motion Transitions are removed when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce is active High contrast Supports Windows High Contrast mode via forced-colors: active File Structure templates/WaynGames.StaticToc.Extension/ ├── partials/ │ ├── head.tmpl.partial # Theme init, fonts, analytics, favicon │ ├── navbar.tmpl.partial # Top nav bar with logo, search, theme toggle │ ├── breadcrumb.tmpl.partial # Version dropdown + breadcrumb trail │ └── scripts.tmpl.partial # DocFX scripts, Highlight.js, Mermaid, version switcher └── styles/ ├── main.css # Full theme (light + dark) and layout overrides └── version-switcher.css # Version bar and dropdown styles Theme System The theme is applied via a data-theme attribute on <html>: On page load, a script in head.tmpl.partial checks localStorage for a saved preference. If none is found, it uses the OS preference via prefers-color-scheme. The navbar toggle button switches between "dark" and "light" and saves the choice. OS-level theme changes are detected in real time and applied if no manual override exists. Color Palette Light mode uses WAYN Games brand colors: Primary (blue): #2968A8 Accent (gold): #C58400 Dark mode adjusts all colors for readability on dark backgrounds: Primary (blue): #5BA3E6 Accent (gold): #E9A84C Both themes include semantic colors for warnings, errors, and success states. How It Extends statictoc The template overrides specific DocFX partials while inheriting the rest from statictoc. DocFX merges templates in order, so WaynGames.StaticToc.Extension only replaces the four partials listed above — all other statictoc behavior (TOC rendering, search index, etc.) remains intact. The build pipeline automatically resolves the template path and passes both statictoc and the extension to docfx build.

manual/versioning.html.title: Versioning | DocTools
manual/versioning.html.summary: Versioning DocTools supports building documentation for multiple versions of a package simultaneously. Each version gets its own output directory, and the generated site includes a dropdown to switch between them. How It Works ALL_VERSIONS in doctools.config lists every version, newest first. CURRENT_VERSION identifies the version that receives C# metadata extraction during builds. Historical versions are built from their own toc.md/index.md snapshots and shared manual content (or per-version overrides). The build produces a versions.js file and a root index.html redirect pointing to the current version. Typical Workflow 1. Start with a single version After doctools.sh init, you have one version (e.g., 0.1.0). Write your documentation and build normally. 2. Snapshot before bumping Before adding a new version, snapshot the current TOC and index so they are preserved: doctools.sh snapshot ./MyPackage This copies Documentation/toc.md and Documentation/index.md into Documentation/v0.1.0/. 3. Add the new version doctools.sh version ./MyPackage add 0.2.0 This: Sets CURRENT_VERSION to 0.2.0 Prepends 0.2.0 to ALL_VERSIONS Creates Documentation/v0.2.0/ with copies of toc.md and index.md 4. Update documentation for the new version Edit the root toc.md, index.md, and manual pages as needed. The v0.1.0/ directory retains the old TOC and index. 5. Build all versions doctools.sh build ./MyPackage all Output: Documentation~/ ├── index.html # Redirects to v0.2.0 ├── versions.js # ["0.2.0", "0.1.0"] ├── v0.2.0/ # Built with metadata └── v0.1.0/ # Built without metadata VERSION_CONTENT Overrides By default, all versions share the same Manual/ directory content. Use VERSION_CONTENT to customize what each version includes. declare -A VERSION_CONTENT VERSION_CONTENT["0.1.0"]="manual_src|manual_exclude|toc_src" Each value has three pipe-delimited components: Component Default Purpose manual_src Manual Directory with manual markdown files manual_exclude Draft/**,Draft V2/** Comma-separated exclude patterns toc_src . Directory containing toc.md and index.md Example: Different TOC per version Store version-specific TOC and index in the version directory: VERSION_CONTENT["0.1.0"]="Manual||v0.1.0" This tells the build to use Documentation/v0.1.0/toc.md and Documentation/v0.1.0/index.md for version 0.1.0, while still pulling manual pages from the shared Manual/ directory. Example: Exclude draft content from a release VERSION_CONTENT["1.0.0"]="|Draft/**,Internal/**|" Version Switcher The generated site includes a version dropdown in the breadcrumb bar. It is populated at runtime from versions.js: window.DOC_VERSIONS = [{"version":"0.2.0"},{"version":"0.1.0"}]; Selecting a different version navigates to ../v{version}/index.html. Directory Structure After several versions, the documentation directory looks like: Documentation/ ├── doctools.config ├── toc.md # Current version TOC ├── index.md # Current version index ├── Manual/ # Shared manual pages │ ├── toc.md │ └── *.md ├── v0.1.0/ # Snapshot │ ├── toc.md │ └── index.md └── v0.2.0/ # Snapshot ├── toc.md └── index.md

manual/workflow.html.title: Package Lifecycle & Documentation Workflow | DocTools
manual/workflow.html.summary: Package Lifecycle & Documentation Workflow This page describes the end-to-end workflow for managing packages with versioned documentation, from initial setup through automated deployment. Package Lifecycle 1. Create package Create a new Unity package with a package.json containing at minimum: name — reverse-domain identifier (e.g., com.wayngames.mypackage) displayName — human-readable title version — semantic version (e.g., 1.0.0) 2. Initialize documentation Run DocTools to scaffold the documentation structure: doctools.sh init ./MyPackage This creates Documentation/ with a table of contents, index page, and DocFX configuration. 3. Write documentation Add markdown pages under Documentation/ Write XML doc comments (/// <summary>) on public C# types for API reference generation Add images to Documentation/Images/ 4. Build locally doctools.sh build ./MyPackage This generates Documentation~/v{X.Y.Z}/ containing the full HTML site with API reference and manual pages. 5. Version bump When preparing a new release: Update version in package.json Update CHANGELOG.md Run doctools.sh build — the new version is built alongside existing versions in Documentation~/ The version switcher dropdown automatically includes all versions found in Documentation~/v*/. 6. Release Push a git tag matching the version: git tag v1.0.0 git push --tags This triggers the automated documentation deployment. Documentation Workflow (CI/CD) When a version tag (v*) is pushed, the following automated flow executes: Package Repo DocTools Packages (gh-pages) | | | |-- tag v1.0.0 pushed --------->| | | docs.yml triggers | | | calls deploy-docs.yml ----->| | | |-- checkout package repo | | |-- checkout DocTools | | |-- install .NET + DocFX | | |-- fetch existing v*/ -------->| | | from gh-pages | | |-- doctools.sh build | | |-- deploy Documentation~/ --->| | | to /{package.name}/ | | | |-- GitHub Pages serves | | | docs.wayn.games/{name}/ Step by step A v* tag push triggers the package repo's .github/workflows/docs.yml docs.yml calls the reusable workflow at WAYN-Games/DocTools/.github/workflows/deploy-docs.yml The reusable workflow: Checks out the package repo and DocTools (private repo, authenticated via PAT) Installs .NET SDK and DocFX Sparse-checkouts previously published version directories from the gh-pages branch of WAYN-Games/Packages into Documentation~/ so the version switcher includes all historical versions Runs doctools.sh build which generates the DocFX site, versions.js, and a root index.html redirect Deploys the full Documentation~/ output to WAYN-Games/Packages gh-pages under /{package.name}/ using keep_files: true (preserving other packages' docs) GitHub Pages serves the content at docs.wayn.games/{package.name}/ The version-switcher dropdown (populated from versions.js) lets users navigate between versions Repository Roles Repository Visibility Role WAYN-Games/DocTools Private Build CLI + reusable GitHub Actions workflow WAYN-Games/Packages Public Hosts gh-pages branch, custom domain docs.wayn.games Package repos Private or Public Source code + doc source, each has a docs.yml caller workflow URL Structure All documentation is served from a single domain: docs.wayn.games/ index.html <- landing page listing all packages com.wayngames.doctools/ index.html <- redirect to latest version versions.js <- version manifest for dropdown v0.1.0/ <- DocFX output for v0.1.0 v0.2.0/ <- DocFX output for v0.2.0 com.wayngamesassets.mgm.charactercontroller/ index.html versions.js v0.3.0/ The URL path segment is the name field from the package's package.json. Adding a New Package Initialize documentation: doctools.sh init ./NewPackage Write docs and build locally: doctools.sh build ./NewPackage Add .github/workflows/docs.yml to the package repo: name: Documentation on: push: tags: ['v*'] workflow_dispatch: jobs: docs: uses: WAYN-Games/DocTools/.github/workflows/deploy-docs.yml@main with: package-name: com.wayngames.newpackage secrets: doctools-token: ${{ secrets.DOCTOOLS_READ_TOKEN }} deploy-token: ${{ secrets.PACKAGES_DEPLOY_TOKEN }} Add DOCTOOLS_READ_TOKEN and PACKAGES_DEPLOY_TOKEN secrets to the repo (or use org-level secrets) Tag a release (git tag v1.0.0 && git push --tags) to trigger deployment Add an entry to landing/index.html in the Packages repo Removing a Version To remove a published version: Locally: doctools.sh version ./PackageDir remove X.Y.Z Re-trigger the docs workflow (via workflow_dispatch) to update versions.js and the root redirect on the deployed site Authentication Setup The system uses two fine-grained Personal Access Tokens for least-privilege access: Secret Name Scope Permission Purpose DOCTOOLS_READ_TOKEN WAYN-Games/DocTools Contents: Read-only Checkout the private build tooling PACKAGES_DEPLOY_TOKEN WAYN-Games/Packages Contents: Read and write Push built docs to gh-pages Both secrets must be added to each package repo (or stored as organization-level secrets for convenience).
